title: Crassula pentandra
intro: Crassula pentandra, also known as the 'Pentandra Crassula' or 'Crassula Pentandra', is a succulent plant species belonging to the family Crassulaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health conditions, with its extracts exhibiting antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Crassula pentandra include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory infections, coughs, and colds.

side_effects: Crassula pentandra is not typically known for its medicinal use, but in general, plants of this genus are considered non-toxic to humans. However, ingesting any plant material can cause stomach upset or allergic reactions in some individuals. Overwatering or root rot associated with Crassula pentandra can lead to the production of toxic compounds that may harm pets or humans if ingested. It's essential to handle the plant with care and keep it out of reach of children and pets to avoid potential issues.
